channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Humanoid robots will work as baggage handlers at Tokyo airport
item.description: Has nobody watched Terminator?

item.title: Families of Tumbler Ridge shooting victims sue OpenAI
item.description: Sam Altman apologized for not notifying local authorities last week.

item.title: Female Looksmaxxer Alorah Ziva Is Suing Clavicular for Alleged Battery
item.description: Aleksandra Mendoza, aka Alorah Ziva, alleges that the 20-year-old influencer injected her with drugs on a livestream and had nonconsensual sex with her while she was underage.

item.title: 'The ocean is really unlimited in terms of how much energy is available': No land, no fuel, no cables — how wave-powered ocean platforms could tackle the power needs of AI data centers
item.description: A startup is developing floating platforms that use wave energy to power offshore AI data centers, reducing emissions, but faces potential durability issues.

item.title: Vortex, la mini-navette de Dassault Aviation pour la nouvelle ère spatiale qui s'ouvre
item.description: L'espace est devenu un domaine contesté. Face à la multiplication des satellites, à l'essor de l'économie orbitale et aux nouvelles menaces, l'Europe cherche à s'affirmer. Dassault Aviation y répond avec le Vortex, un projet de mini-navette spatiale réutilisable développé discrètement avec le...

item.title: I tried Google’s new AI wardrobe feature with Motorola’s Razr — and it turns your photos into a surprisingly useful digital closet full of outfits
item.description: Google Photos’ new AI wardrobe feature builds a digital closet from your photos, organizes outfits, and even offers virtual try-ons. I tried it on Motorola’s new Razr devices, and it’s a surprisingly compelling look at how AI could reshape your camera roll.

item.title: I've hand-picked the best Lego Botanicals sets for Mother's Day, from spiky succulents to cheerful sunflowers
item.description: Lego's Botanicals range has bloomed to include plants of all kinds, and your mom's favorite is almost certainly included.

item.title: IBM launches Bob with multi-model routing and human checkpoints to turn AI coding into a secure production system
item.description: Bringing AI agents into the enterprise software development lifecycle is fast becoming the norm. As developers experiment with new platforms, organizations are exposed to potential security and orchestration failures. Systems that work in pilots may fail once the agents start working with real-time data.Legacy tech giant IBM is one of several companies trying to address that gap by introducing more structure into how these workflows run. Yesterday, it announced the global launch of its AI-powered software development platform Bob, designed to write and test code across the development cycle, a...

item.title: Parallel Web Systems hits $2B valuation five months after its last big raise
item.description: The AI agent-tool startup founded by former Twitter CEO Parag Agrawal has raised $100 million, led by Sequoia, months after raising a previous $100 million.

item.title: Motorola’s newest Razrs add battery life but subtract more from your wallet
item.description: Motorola Razr 2026 lineup adds battery improvements but comes with higher prices across models.

item.title: AWS Quick's personal knowledge graph is making orchestration decisions most control planes can't see
item.description: Enterprise AI teams running centralized orchestration stacks now have a new variable to account for: AWS Quick, which expanded this week to a desktop-native agent that builds a persistent personal knowledge graph and executes actions across local files and SaaS tools — outside the visibility of most control planes.Unlike chat-based copilots that reset with each session, Quick now maintains a continuously updated knowledge graph built from the user's local files, calendar, email and connected SaaS apps. It uses it to proactively trigger actions without waiting to be asked.AWS launched Quick in ...

item.title: The EU thinks Meta isn't doing enough to protect children
item.description: A preliminary investigation found the company isn't adequately keeping minors off of Instagram and Facebook.

item.title: Is this 'the largest breach in football history'? Hackers allegedly breach Cristiano Ronaldo's Saudi team and AFC governing body, leak passports, contracts, and emails online
item.description: More than 150,000 people allegedly affected, including tens of thousands of professional athletes.

item.title: Emily Blunt tells young women to quit their terrible jobs. Not everyone can afford to follow the advice
item.description: The ‘Devil Wears Prada 2’ actress, whose character famously muttered ‘I love my job’ during a moment of stress, sparked a debate about toxic workplaces. When The Devil Wears Prada debuted in 2006, it introduced the world to cerulean blue and the not-so-glam life of fashion and editorial.

item.title: 15 quirky gadgets for your desk — and yes, they’re actually really useful for work
item.description: As a gadget-lover, I found some cool office gear that's practical and fun.

item.title: All the evidence unveiled so far in Musk v. Altman
item.description: The Musk v. Altman trial is underway, and that means exhibits, or the evidence to be presented in court, are being revealed piece by piece. So far, email exchanges, photos, and corporate documents are circulating from the earliest days of OpenAI - and from before the AI lab even had a name. Some high-level takeaways: […]

item.title: This smart fridge scans your food and your fingerprints to give you personalized nutrition advice — but I'm not sure I want judgemental appliances in my kitchen
item.description: The refrigerator has a robotic arm to organize your food, and uses biometric data to help you meet your health goals.

item.title: Stranger Things: Tales from 85: Ending explainer
item.description: Who created the Queen? What happens to Nikki? What's next for Season 2? Your "Stranger Things: Tales from '85" questions, answered.

item.title: THX ouvre deux nouveaux laboratoires en Asie pour certifier la qualité d’image et celle du son
item.description: THX Ltd vient d’annoncer l’ouverture de nouveaux centres de certification à Shenzhen et Taipei, avec Shenzhen comme unique centre agréé HDR10+ et Eclipsa Audio en Chine.

item.title: Unique Mothers Day gift idea alert: These Bouqs flower bouquets come bundled with matching Lego sets
item.description: The limited-edition Bouqs Co. x Lego Botanicals collection bundles real flowers with matching Lego builds. They make a great unique Mother's Day gift.
